desc:
This small yard, with here and there a tussock of low grass, ends at a wooden
barn to the north and a small house to the south. Near the barn, within an
enclosure of wooden poles, some pigs enjoy the mud, while at the other end a
large pile of wood, chopped into small blocks is placed against the wall of the
small house. From the west the smell of decomposing plants enters the yard,
while to the east the yard continues.
name: Farmyard

desc:
The trail ends in an open space with a small house to the south. The space
itself is covered in dust with here and there a tussock of grass or a lonely
weed. A shallow water-well, covered with a reed thatched roof, has been erected
in the southeastern corner of the yard. This well provides the local
inhabitants, their domesticated animals and the seedbeds with young vegetables
ready to be replanted in surrounding fields with water.
name: Open Space

desc:
A small but well-maintained and well-furnished house features a big, warm
fireplace with deer pelts around it. Dried leaves are used to decorate the
windows. Around the room various baskets, at different stages of completion, lie
about. A workbench with typical basket weaver tools is placed beneath one of the
windows. A screen partially blocks the way to an area in the back of the house.
name: Basketmaker's House

desc:
This small room is separated from the main part of the house by a screen. A
large, brown fur covers most of the floor and the walls were constructed using
thick wooden beams. A small window shuttered by wooden planks provides the room
with some daylight. A pile of old, dusty clothes has been piled up in the far
corner of the room, and a sturdy bed is placed under the small window.
name: Basketmaker's Bedroom

desc:
Small windows made in the thick wooden walls enable the sunlight to penetrate to
the inside of the building. In the corner near the stove hams are hanging on
iron hooks, which in turn hang from the straw-thatched roof. The floor is
covered with baked red tiles, except for the cellar door. Some furniture,
obviously old, is placed in the house.
name: Farm House

desc:
Built underneath one of the buildings from the farm, this basement seems to
serve as some kind of storage facility. Wooden planks, filled with jars, have
been attached to the stone walls. Some of the jars have yellowish labels on them
describing their contents. The floor is mostly unoccupied except for a large
wooden chest in the far corner. A hatch with a ladder leads to the house above.
name: Musty Cellar

desc:
This large wooden barn is sectioned into several compartments by low wooden
walls. The back compartments are filled with piles of rye. Those more to the
front contain baskets with vegetables like carrots and cabbage, while the front
one, left of the entrance is equipped with a crib and a trough filled with
water. Right of the entrance a wooden bench with various tools, like sickle and
pitchfork, hanging next to it, marks the workroom of the local farmer.
name: Wooden Barn

desc:
Some bushes and small trees grow here. Among these a few patches of thistles and
some scattered ground-level thrive. Around the flourishing bushes a number of
small birds fly around, picking up the occasional insect hiding between the
leaves. The ground gradually slopes down toward a small pond to the south. Some
winding animal tracks head in that direction.
name: Field by a Pond

desc:
Over time a small depression has developed in these grasslands. Rainwater from
the surrounding fields drains to there forming a large, tranquil pond. This pond
has a distinctive green colour from algae that exists among the water- lilies
growing along the shoreline. Despite being surrounded by reeds a small small
path leading to the waterfront, can be distinguished.
name: Pond in the Fields

desc:
The fields here are overgrown with thistles, shrubs and bushes. Standing in the
middle of the fields, the weather-beaten remains of an old building can be seen.
An old, fading trail leads from the village remains to the east, across the
fields and down the gentle westward slope to a small pond.
name: Overgrown Fields

desc:
An open plain stretches from east to west, bordered in the north by some lone
birch trees in a soggy loam. The plain provides little in the form of shelter
from the elements or predators. Nevertheless, faint trails of animal tracks,
most likely deer, criss-cross through the open landscape.
name: Plains

desc:
Soot covered walls are all that remains of this small village. Once a place for
farmers from the surrounding areas to gather, it seems to have been abandoned
for many years now. Weeds and bracken have spread past the gardens surrounding
the derelict buildings, adding to the sombreness of the location. A few small
animals scurry among the debris, seeking cover from some unseen threat.
name: An Abandoned Village

desc:
Thick bushes grow among the grasses in this stretch of the plains. Some twisted
animal paths can be clearly distinguished, interweaving among the tightly packed
bushes. The ground slopes downwards slightly to the north leading to a soggy
area. Scattered among the bushes a few slender plants with bluish petals grow.
Small animals scurry about, alert to the slightest sign of a predator. Some
buildings have been erected in the distance to the south.
name: Plains North of a Village

desc:
Lush, green grass now covers the abandoned fields that lie next to the village
in the west. The earth rises slightly to the south, elevating as it turns into
vast grasslands. To the northeast, hills rise above the plains providing the
most salient landmark in the terrain, as well as a vantage point over these
extensive plains.
name: Grassland by the Village

desc:
The battered fencing that surrounds these fields is the clearest evidence that
whoever claimed ownership of them has long ago given up. Thistles, scattered
shrubs and bushes grow sparsely in the area covering up any other evidence of
farming activity. To the east a tall hill rises above the plains while to the
southwest the remains of a small village dot the horizon.
name: An Abandoned Field

desc:
This lush valley has a rich vegetation dotted with many different coloured
flowers. This provides an environment that is favoured not only by large grazing
mammals but also by small rodents and insects. A few scattered bones suggest
that predators also find an occasional meal in this location.
name: A Flowering Vale

desc:
Navigating these hills requires not much climbing. Sparsely distributed along
the hillside oak, ash and birch trees grow and among these several shrubs and
bushes. To the east the ground rises, while the northern and southern slopes
lead to small valleys in the plains. Among the bushes a worn trail indicates
significant transit of some large animals into a small creek among the hills.
name: Lightly-wooded Hills

desc:
The top plateau of the Great Hill is small, rocky, and contains a very limited
amount of low bushes and stunted grass. The wind has no obstacles here, and it
occasionally blows hard, making the sparse vegetation of plateau to bend. The
plateau offers a truly magnificent view of the surrounding areas. Looking
around, slopes and valleys encircle the hills from all sides. Looking beyond
those, vast plains continue in all directions as far as one can see.
name: Great Hill

desc:
This deep valley lies peacefully beneath an enormous hillside, beckoning the
travellers to settle down upon it. The towering hill completely blocks the view
northwards. The inclined, rocky wall makes a perfect home for a great number of
birds, and some of them can be seen circling the sky. A tiny path runs through
the elevating plains to the east and west, peacefully winding in the shadows
cast by the high hills.
name: Valley in the Plains

desc:
The sloping ground inclines gradually throughout the whole area. The surface of
the ground is rather grim: dark, grey stones are scattered around the slope, and
the short, stunted grass does not offer much comfort to passing travellers. No
trees are visible on the slope, as a narrow path can be seen descending from the
northern hills towards the more fertile lands to the west. To the east, the
ground is too steep and rocky for travelling.
name: Sloping Plains

desc:
High on the hills, an old path slithers through the rough terrain, descending
dangerously towards a slope in the north. There are rocks, stones, and boulders
on both sides of the path, which limit the view from here. There are some old
tracks on the path and the blackened remains of a few abandoned campfires.
name: High Hills

desc:
This flat plain is covered entirely with tall, dense grasses, which sway back
and forth as a cold breeze passes by. There is a small path slithering through
the thick, rolling fields. The landscape is dominated by high hills, which can
be spotted not far to the west. The open plain continues in all directions, with
the exception of the west, where a steep incline, filled with boulders, prevents
any further travel.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The barren, flat plain is covered with tangled, yellowish grass tussocks. Large
areas are entirely dry, where grass is coloured almost orange. Only a few trees
are visible on the whole plain, and every single one of them looks grey, dry,
and dead. A huge hill is visible to the northwest, but a steep ridge blocks the
direct route there, forcing travellers to use a detour.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The feral plain opens up in all directions. The ground winds up and down,
forming small mounds. The surface of the plain is covered by messy, bright green
bushes, and light grey, nearly white, stones, which are strangely grouped
together into small piles. Some high hills can be spotted to the west, whereas
the plain seems to end further to the east where it gets progressively rocky.
name: Rocky Plains

desc:
A foot long, brown-green grass covers the plain, forming a slowly winding sea
around the clumps of thick bushes that grow in great numbers around the area.
The eastern part of the plain holds vegetation so dense, that it is impossible
to go any further in that direction. A large number of small stones forms the
remains of a rarely used path through the field, which cuts the sea of grass
into separate areas.
name: Brushy Grassland

desc:
Dry, yellow-brown grass covers the central parts of this wide plain. The sea of
short straws is bent and cracked from the occasional gusts of wind that has
blown across this shrivelled landscape. Brush and thick bushes border the
windswept plain to the south and west, blocking the passage effectively, while
the plains extend in all other directions.
name: Withered Plains

desc:
The grassland peters out in this gaunt, windswept landscape. Everything is very
dry, and there are a few large blackened areas, where all the grass and other
undergrowth have totally been burnt away. A few bare trees are still standing on
the field, their trunks twisted and blackened, and their leaves having
shrivelled and fallen off. This bleak barrenness continues to the north and
west.
name: Arid Plains

desc:
This extensive tract of uncultivated open land is covered with herbage and low
shrubs, like raspberries, brambles and spiky bushes, which form occasional
bright green spots in the otherwise somewhat insipid undergrowth. The terrain
looks modest, and harmless. Several foot-high fertile grasses surround this
peaceful plain. To the east, the grasses grows gradually longer, and have a
browner hue.
name: Tranquil Plain

desc:
This is a large, rocky area composed of narrow passes between steep rock faces;
coarse cliffs filled with dense briar thickets; and rounded boulders exposed by
erosion. Some of the rocks are half-buried in the ocher clay; some of them are
laying upon each other in dangerous looking, unbalanced manner. There are deep
burrows among the rocks, making it perilous to proceed further between the
boulders. The only easy way out is the green plain that is visible to the east.
name: Rocky Area

desc:
This rugged cavern has been formed by nature ages ago. The pathway makes a
confusing and narrow route between the dull edged stalagmites which protrude
from the rocky and uneven floor. The sheer rock walls of this crude cavern are
slimy and moist, and some pearls of water are visible on the stalagmites as
well. The cold, damp air hangs still.
name: Encompassed in the Damp Darkness

desc:
Vegetation in the area is primarily low bush and scrub. The land is mostly
without trees; instead a lot of tall, yellow grass grow here. Amongst it,
occasional patches of foot-high green grass make the landscape look vivid and
colourful. No clear path exists in any direction; on the other hand, the terrain
is so easy that pasturing animals can just wander wherever they want.
name: Plains of Yellow Grass

desc:
Various vigorous, shrubby plants dominate this area, blooming in rich shades of
yellow, orange, red, and crimson. Around them, large blue and deep purple
flowers make vivid colour patches that stand out from the mottled undergrowth,
which is painted in uncountable slight variations of green, brown, and even
grey.
name: Colourful Plains

desc:
Rigid, dense grass spreads across the landscape here, cloaking everything in
deep green. The terrain is mostly level, with a few grassy mounds scattered
throughout. The soil looks sunburnt but fertile; among the grass a number of
herbaceous plants are showing new bright green leaves. To the east, a long hedge
of thick bramble bushes separates two grassy plains from each other.
name: Undisturbed Grasslands

desc:
This flowery and fertile land consists of scattered trees within a continuous
vast, flat grassland. The grass has an auburn-tinted hue to it, and on several
spots it appears to have been trampled on. A couple of worn stone boulders are
standing in the sea of grass. Numerous white flowers grow around the rocks,
adorning the area. Lightly rolling mats of grass and forbs stretch onward in
every direction.
name: Auburn Fields

desc:
Lush grasses and small plants have occupied this area. The ground elevates
slightly, creating small mounds covered in green grass with only a few small
bushes with narrow leaves growing on the ground. The general lack of bigger
trees suggests that this is a land of little moisture. The plain continues in
gentle slopes to all directions, granting clear and unobstructed visibility.
name: Mounding Plains

desc:
The soil is porous and fertile; a well developed grass stratum and a few trees
of low height grow on it. A wide zone of thick bramble bushes has formed a broad
and tall natural hedge that cuts the passage to the west. The tranquil open
plains continue in other directions, allowing easy and clear travelling. The
ground is filled with small fragrant flowers and plants.
name: Natural Hedge

desc:
Perched within the folds of the lonely, parched tree, the thin branches spread
out in all directions. There is scarcely any foliage on the tree, only a few
dry, narrow leaves cling to the tips of the fragile branches. The tree is a good
and obvious vantage point. It offers a clear view of the surrounding lands. The
vast grassland continues in every direction as far as the eye can see except to
the north where the murky Mirkwood looms.
name: Up in a Tree

desc:
The eastern end of the wide plain transforms into a rocky area filled with
stones and boulders of all sizes. A long, bushy cliff stretches through the
area, twisting and branching, forming a confusing sprawl of sheer rock faces,
irregularly shaped boulders, and tangled, dark bushes. In most places, both
visibility and travel is blocked by the eastern boulders.
name: Rocky Area

desc:
Concealed from the eyes of the common wanderers, a hidden path runs inside the
bushy vegetation. The way is narrow, but provides enough room to allow one to
rest for a while, or even to make a temporary camp here. The southern exit has
been camouflaged with loose bushes. It must have happened some time ago, as the
bushes have withered and died.
name: Hidden Path

desc:
The cavern is filled with large, grey rocks which lie scattered around. At some
places the floor is plain, smooth rock, elsewhere it is filled with sand and
gravel. There is an obscure pathway which flits its way around the boulders,
rocks and winding walls, creating a confusing, but fairly passable route
throughout the barren area. At one end, the cave ascends and finally leads up to
a wide ledge and a massive boulder.
name: End of a Rocky Cavern

desc:
A round, rocky cave rises up into great heights. It is filled with boulders and
rocks, intact stonewalls and crude gravel. There are plenty of shady ledges,
overhangs and other obstacles formed of stone that creates numerous hideouts and
places to lurk in the shadowy darkness.
name: Rocky Cave

desc:
A wide cavern opens up in the underground. The place is full of filth and mess,
and emits a foul stench. Roughly constructed, giant sized furniture have been
made of rocks and tree logs. There are a few sitting places and a stone ledge
working as a table. Old bones, broken wine jars, and other junk lay scattered
around the cavern. A few dark alcoves in the stonewall seem to be serving as
sleeping areas. One exit leads down via a dark hole in the floor, while a
massive, old padlock is used to secure an obstruction made of rock and stone to
the east.
name: Deep inside the Ground

desc:
A windy, dark tunnel shrivels inside the ground. At some places the solid stone
walls are near, sometimes they are far from each other. The floor is made of
cold rock as well. While the ceiling and most sections of the walls are rather
uneven, the floor seems smooth in most places and bears the signs of having been
worn out and smoothened by countless heavy feet over a long time.
name: Underground Tunnel

desc:
Behind the heavy boulder, a twisted tunnel runs towards the darkness. The air
appears to be rather damp. Although it is very dark, there is visible evidence
of odd tracks on the cold earth floor. The walls are leaning towards each other,
and the space between them gets narrower and narrower when one proceeds forward.
The tunnel ends at a wider cavern where several shattered rocks and some old
bones lay scattered on the dirt floor around a dark hole leading down.
name: Narrow Tunnel

desc:
This cellar had originally been constructed using large granite blocks, but now
the partly collapsed walls have been temporarily repaired by rough wooden
planks. To allow storage of clothing and other items large iron nails have been
hammered into the planks. On the top shelves in the back corner some small boxes
and jars, containing various foods, have been placed, while the lower shelves
are filled with various tools.
name: Hidden Cellar

desc:
The grass stands almost two feet high, bending to any wind that may blow on it.
The greenery spreads its influence strongly to the south, while the northern
parts of the field appear less fruitful. A scorched fence encircles a burned out
farm in the east, and in the west an esker marks the horizon.
name: Flowing Meadows

desc:
A small, dusty path makes it way to a burned-out farm. The scent of decaying
carcasses befouls the air. There are remainders of what could have been a stable
lying all across the pathway. To the south stands the sooty skeleton of what
appears to have been an old farmhouse. An old fence encircles the farm, and
behind it the plains open up to the west. A heavy brush area grows every so
close to the east, slowly approaching the farm.
name: A Path before a Farm

desc:
The meadows are covered with grass, closing in to the ridge stretching from the
north and southwards. Long lines of perfectly rounded pebbles and stones line
the stone ridge. In the north the feet high grass slowly disappears, replaced by
barren fields, and in the south the meadows turn into a vast steppe, while in
the east the undergrowth grows thicker.
name: A Ridge in the Meadows

desc:
A layer of salt covers the entire ground, preventing anything from growing here.
The plains are mostly covered with stones, varying from small pebbles to a
handful of major boulders. Far away in the east the earthy ground appears to be
overwhelmed by rocks, while in the north and south the malnourished plains
expand ruthlessly.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
An ridge disrupts the flat steppe, rising high and steep from the ground.
Rounded pebbles and rocks lie scattered along the stone ridge. Grass has slowly
turned the dried-out surroundings green. The steppe gradually turns into a more
vivid prairie to the east, a flowing meadow to the north, and back to its own
pale self to the west.
name: A Ridge through the Steppe

desc:
A flat, lonely landscape spreads far away towards meadows in the north, and more
plains in the west. The horizon in the east is marked by a heightened esker in
the steppe. The grass here grows to almost one foot in height, and adds a slight
nuance of colour to the otherwise-barren area.
name: Vast Steppe

desc:
A dull, ashen landscape stretches far in all directions. The pale colour of the
earth is due to the layer of salt which covers it, preventing anything from
growing here. Pebbles and rocks lying scattered across the cracked ground are
the only things giving this land shape. A vast steppe opens up to the east.
name: Barren Plains

desc:
The grass stands a few feet tall, growing quite dense. Small bushes and a sole
tree grow here, nourished by the rich soil. The prairie continues in the east,
while in the west the landscape becomes grey, turning into a vast steppe. A
farmhouse has been heavily ravaged, barely standing behind its picket fence in
the north.
name: A Deserted Prairie

desc:
Scrawny trees and bushes of different sizes are slowly taking over more and more
land, disrupting the prevalence of flowing grass. A small snake pit lies poorly
hidden under some rocks, with obvious traces of snakes slithering to and fro the
pit. The vegetation grows thicker in the east and north, while in the west it
appears to be more or less the same as here.
name: A Deserted Prairie

desc:
Undergrowth and foliage are very abundant here, spanning from the south into the
east. Poison ivy uses the burned-out fence in the west as its foundation. In the
north the great forest of Mirkwood leans over the thorny brushland, creating an
impermeable wall of vegetation. An abandoned fox den lies wide open beneath a
clump of shrubs. It appears to have been ravaged by another animal. Remnants of
small animals, doubtless those who fell prey to foxes, lie scattered on the
ground.
name: Light Brushland

desc:
The roof has partly collapsed due to damage from a fire. The southern wall still
stands, and an old skeleton reclines against it. The door is damaged, and is
just barely hanging from its hinges. The walls to the east and north carry what
is left of the ceiling. The vast meadows can be seen through the western window.
Vegetation has slowly started to creep into this area, with vines climbing
through the cracked windows and walls.
name: A Deteriorating Farmhouse

desc:
A myriad of rotting branches and leaves are spread around, almost covering up
the moss and gravel on the uneven ground. The cramped together trees, nestled
with vines and fallen branches, block further travel in all directions except
for to the east, where the trees seem to grow further apart.
name: Mirkwood Forest

desc:
An open, meadow-like landscape grows mainly brown heather, which have gathered
among the rocks covered by pale green moss, and around the roots of the few
existing trees. Most of the vegetation that covers the ground is dried grass,
although some small plants with glossy, green leaves rise from it. To the west,
a boulder-strewn area can be seen, while in other directions the vegetation
looks more fertile and is flourishing.
name: Plain of Heather

desc:
Underneath the leaves, in the thickest shrubbery, runs a narrow but straight and
otherwise decent thoroughfare. In the heart of the thicket, the path widens and
forms a small clearing growing soft, bright green grass. This place is well
covered from the external world and could be used as a place to take a rest, or
as a secret hideout. The path continues to south, whereas the entrance here has
been hidden among the bushes.
name: Inside the Vegetation
